summary.intent: Implement a guard in cloud mode that rejects repository evaluations exceeding 1,000,000 lines of code, leveraging existing cloc output, with a configurable limit and a friendly frontend error message.
summary.outcome: All four planned changes were implemented, lint errors were fixed, all 1324 tests passed, and the changes were committed as a single focused commit staging only the 4 relevant files.

repo: cloc runs during context identification and exposes clocOutput (JSON) with SUM.code as total LOC — available on contextResult without any new tooling
job-manager.ts:434 already extracts error.code from thrown errors, so custom error codes propagate automatically
The API route class exposes this.cloudMode, following the same pattern as other evaluation options like verbose and debug
The linter enforces noExplicitAny — (error as any).code casts are not allowed; a typed interface or custom error class is required instead
bun run lint:fix auto-corrects formatting issues; bun run lint alone only reports them

code.finding: cloudMode?: boolean added to IEvaluationOptions interface
cloudMode: this.cloudMode passed into body.options alongside verbose, debug, etc.

LOC guard block: parses clocOutput JSON, checks SUM.code against MAX_REPO_LOC env var (default 1_000_000), throws typed error with code REPO_TOO_LARGE; malformed cloc output is fail-safe (evaluation proceeds)
Friendly error block matching 'repository too large' string, returns title/description/suggestion with CLI workaround hint

workflow: git commands are run via the rtk prefix (likely a remote tunnel wrapper)
When unrelated unstaged changes exist, stage only task-specific files explicitly rather than using git add -A to keep commits focused
Run lint and tests together in one command (bun run lint && bun run test) for a fast final verification pass

friction: Initial implementation used (error as any).code casts which violated the noExplicitAny lint rule, requiring a second edit pass
Indentation was also off in the first engine.ts edit, requiring a full block replacement
The repo had many pre-existing unstaged/untracked changes unrelated to the task, requiring careful selective staging
open_items: Manual verification steps from the plan were not performed in-session: cloud mode rejection test, non-cloud passthrough test, and MAX_REPO_LOC threshold override test
Pre-existing modified and untracked files in the repo remain uncommitted and may need attention separately
